data.title: From rockets to radar, the aerospace and defense industry soars in 2025
data.description:
The aerospace and defense industry, a cornerstone of the industrials sector, delivered an exceptional showing in 2025, handily outperforming both its parent sector and the broader equity market.

Supported by sustained defense spending, rising commercial aerospace demand, and expanding space-related investment, the group benefited from powerful tailwinds throughout the year.

As the calendar turns toward year-end, aerospace and defense stands out as one of the market’s strongest performers, with the industry being +48.5% year to date. That gain far exceeds the industrials sector’s +18.5% advance and comfortably surpasses the S&P 500’s (SP500) +17% rise, underscoring the group’s relative strength.

Within this favorable environment, several companies delivered outsized returns. The list below highlights the 10 best-performing U.S.-listed aerospace and defense stocks of 2025, focusing exclusively on companies with market capitalizations of at least $1 billion. 

No. 1: Astronics (ATRO), +246.8% YTD.

No. 2: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions (KTOS), +212.0% YTD.

No. 3: Rocket Lab (RKLB), +203.0% YTD.

No. 4: ATI (ATI), +111.1% YTD. 

No. 5: Carpenter Technology (CRS), +96.5% YTD.

No. 6: Howmet Aerospace (HWM), +91.6% YTD. 

No. 7: Woodward (WWD), +90.0% YTD.

No. 8: General Electric (GE), +89.2% YTD. 

No. 9: VSE Corporation (VSEC), +88.1% YTD. 

No. 10: Huntington Ingalls Industries (HII), +87.6% YTD. 

Aerospace & Defense: (ITA), (PPA), (XAR), (SHLD), (FITE), and (DFEN).

Industrials ETFs: (XLI), (VIS), (FXR), (IYJ), (FIDU), and (RSPN).

data.title: Eastern International announces photovoltaic power generation construction project contract
data.description:
• Eastern International (ELOG) on Wednesday said its wholly-owned subsidiary, Guizhou Tianrun Zhicheng Construction Engineering has entered into a construction contract with Weifang Branch of CSCEC Southwest Architecture & Design Institute Design Consulting for its Hebei Laiyuan 300 MW Centralized Photovoltaic Power Generation Project.
  • Pursuant to the contract, Guizhou Tianrun will act as a subcontractor and be responsible for the construction of Phase I - 50 MW of the Centralized Photovoltaic Power Generation Project, with a total contract price of RMB 42.5 million.
  • Guizhou Tianrun will be responsible for the foundation works, photovoltaic mounting structure installation, photovoltaic module installation, module string wiring, installation and wiring of photovoltaic combiner boxes and supply of certain auxiliary materials.
  • The project is scheduled to commence for construction before December 31, 2025.
  • ELOG +42.95% premarket to $2.03.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: South Korea, U.S. move toward nuclear submarine cooperation deal
data.description:
South Korea and the United States have agreed to begin work toward a standalone accord that would explicitly recognize Seoul’s ability to develop nuclear-powered submarines, Bloomberg News reported Wednesday, citing South Korea’s national security adviser. The talks signal a potential shift in long-standing nuclear restrictions.

Speaking after meetings in Washington, Wi Sung-lac said the two sides concurred that a separate agreement would be required to advance cooperation on nuclear-powered submarines, with working-level discussions expected to start early next year. Wi said he raised the issue in talks with senior U.S. officials, including Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Energy Secretary Chris Wright.

The visit was intended to speed implementation of pledges outlined in a joint fact sheet released following an October summit between South Korean President Lee Jae Myung and U.S. President Donald Trump. That document referenced expanded cooperation on areas including enriched uranium, spent nuclear fuel reprocessing and submarine propulsion.

A U.S. working-level delegation is expected to travel to Seoul early next year to continue follow-up talks, Wi said, with both governments planning to establish benchmarks for reviewing progress later in the year.

South Korea’s defense minister has said the government hopes to wrap up negotiations with Washington within two years on arrangements to secure nuclear fuel supplies for submarines.

data.title: Stock futures inch up ahead of truncated Christmas Eve trading session
data.description:
U.S. stock index futures were marginally higher on Wednesday, ahead of a shortened regular trading session due to Christmas Eve.

Markets will close at 1300 ET today and will be shut on Thursday.

Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) rose +0.13%. S&P 500 futures (SPX) advanced +0.11%, and Dow futures (INDU) edged higher by +0.07%.

Traders received some labor market data in the form of an unexpectedly lower initial jobless claims report. 

A blowout U.S. Q3 GDP report lifted sentiment in the previous session and helped Wall Street notch its 38th record close of the year, underscoring the stock market's resilience after a turbulent 2025.

See the chart below shared by Charlie Bilello, chief market strategist at Creative Planning, on X:

Wednesday also marks the start of a period that has traditionally resulted in gains for equities, leading to a Santa Claus rally - a historical trend that has seen the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500) post gains during the last five trading days of the year and the first two of the next year.  

U.S. Treasury yields were mixed in early trading. The shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) rose 0.8 basis points to 3.54%, while the benchmark U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) slipped 0.2 basis points to 4.17%. The U.S. 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) ticked up 0.1 basis points to 4.83%.

Top S&P 500 gainers in premarket trading included NIKE (NKE) +2.37%, Microchip Technology (MCHP) +1.76%, and Solstice Advanced Materials (SOLS) +1.54%. Decliners included Intel (INTC) -3.27%, LKQ (LKQ) -2.77%, and CoStar (CSGP) -1.70%.

data.title: Initial jobless claims surprise lower in Dec. 20 week; continuing claims rise
data.description:
Initial jobless claims for the week ended Dec. 20 fell by 10K to 214K, vs. 224K consensus and 224K prior (unrevised), according to data released by the Department of Labor on Wednesday.

Meanwhile, continuing claims of 1.923M in the week ended Dec. 13 rose from the prior week's 1.885M, which was revised from 1.897M.

data.title: Cerence announces repurchase of $30 million of 1.50% convertible senior notes due 2028
data.description:
• Cerence (CRNC) has agreed to repurchase $30 million aggregate principal amount of 1.50% convertible senior notes due 2028 from holders at a cash repurchase price equal to 92% of their principal amount.
  • The transaction will reduce interest expense, eliminate potential dilution from refinancing the notes being repurchased, and lower leverage – intended to deliver a net positive outcome for shareholders, the company said.
  • CRNC +0.62% premarket to $11.37.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: AST SpaceMobile shares rise after successful BlueBird 6 launch in LEO
data.description:
Shares of AST SpaceMobile (ASTS) are up in premarket trading on Wednesday following the successful launch of BlueBird 6, the largest satellite ever to be deployed in low earth orbit.

ASTS is up 2.7% at $87.98 on the NASDAQ before markets opened.

BlueBird 6 is capable of delivering high-speed 4G and 5G directly to standard, unmodified smartphones and government devices from space, the company said. 

The satellite, which is thrice the size and has 10 times the capacity of its six satellites currently in orbit, launched from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre in Sriharikota, India, on a Launch Vehicle Mark-3 rocket.

"The satellite is designed to enable peak data rates of up to 120 Mbps directly to standard, unmodified mobile devices, supporting voice, full data, and video applications from space," AST SpaceMobile said.

The satellite was assembled, integrated, and tested at the company's manufacturing facilities in Midland, Texas. The company continues to be on track to launch 45–60 satellites by the end of 2026, with launches planned every one or two months on average.

data.title: Valkea announces private placement of up to $2.5 million
data.description:
• Valkea Resources (OZ:CA) has launched a non-brokered private placement of up to 10,000,000 units of the company at a price of C$0.25 per unit for aggregate gross proceeds of up to C$2.5M.
  • Each unit will be comprised of one common share of the company and one-half of one share purchase warrant. Each warrant will entitle the holder to purchase one share at an exercise price of C$0.35 for a period of 36 months following the date of issuance.
  • The private placement is expected to close on or about January 15, 2026.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: DLC Holdings to sell White River macadamia factory for $1.2M
data.description:
• DLC Holdings (DLC:CA) has agreed to sell its White River macadamia processing facility in Mpumalanga, South Africa for $1.2M.
  • The sale agreement is dated December 19, 2025, and the asset is owned through DLC’s wholly owned subsidiary, Superior Macadamias Ltd.
  • Sale proceeds will be paid on deferred terms, with $500,000 expected by the end of 2025 and the remaining balance payable during 2026.
  • DLC plans to use the proceeds to repay outstanding third-party loans previously disclosed by the Company.

data.title: Trump reportedly seeks finalization of marijuana rescheduling by January-end
data.description:
Following his recent executive order seeking a reclassification of marijuana as a less dangerous drug, President Donald Trump has directed Attorney General Pam Bondi to issue a final ruling on rescheduling by the end of January, The Marijuana Herald reported.

However, citing sources close to the president, the publication added that the ruling could come as early as next month, even though Trump’s order to Bondi suggests a final decision by the end of January.

Cannabis stocks surged last week ahead of Trump’s order, which seeks the inclusion of marijuana alongside medicines such as ketamine under Schedule III of the Controlled Substances Act and removal from its current classification under Schedule I, where dangerous drugs such as LSD are placed. 

The directive requires Bondi “to take all necessary steps to complete the rulemaking process related to rescheduling marijuana to Schedule III of the CSA in the most expeditious manner in accordance with Federal law.”

If the Department of Justice follows through with a final ruling by the end of next month, marijuana would be officially classified as a Schedule III drug by the spring.

While it will not legalize marijuana for recreational purposes federally, U.S. cannabis firms are expected to gain benefits such as the elimination of a punitive tax provision called 280E that they had to contend with for decades.

U.S. multi-state operators: Curaleaf Holdings (CURLF), Ascend Wellness (AAWH), Cresco Labs (CRLBF), Trulieve Cannabis (TCNNF), Green Thumb Industries (GTBIF), Jushi Holdings (JUSHF), and Verano Holdings (VRNO)

Canadian Licensed Producers: Canopy Growth (CGC), Tilray (TLRY), Cronos (CRON), Aurora Cannabis (ACB), SNDL Inc. (SNDL), and OrganiGram Holdings (OGI)

Cannabis ETFs: AdvisorShares Pure US Cannabis ETF (MSOS), Amplify Seymour Cannabis ETF (CNBS), Amplify Alternative Harvest ETF (MJ), and AdvisorShares Pure Cannabis ETF (YOLO).
